subject: English
skill: Multisyllable Word Reading and Advanced Phonics

lesson.story_mission: Join the Word Rangers in the Echo Library. Each locked shelf opens when you read a longer word by chunks instead of guessing.
lesson.objective: I can decode multisyllable words with open and closed syllables, vowel teams, r-controlled vowels, prefixes, and suffixes.
lesson.concept_explanation: Longer words become easier when readers slow down and find chunks. An open syllable ends with a vowel that often says its long sound, a closed syllable has a vowel closed in by a consonant, r-controlled vowels like ar, er, ir, or, and ur stay together, and prefixes or suffixes add meaning to a base word.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may guess from the first few letters, split a vowel-r team, or miss the meaning of a prefix or suffix.
lesson.real_world_connection: These strategies help readers solve new words in chapter books, science articles, directions, and classroom projects.
lesson.lesson_summary: Find the word parts, mark the vowel pattern, read each chunk, blend the chunks, and check the meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: First look for prefixes and suffixes. Next mark vowel patterns and syllables. Then read each chunk and blend the whole word.

audio.text: syllable, r-controlled vowel, prefix, suffix, word part
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Say each reading word with me.

worked_examples.text: Watch: robot splits ro | bot. The first syllable is open, so ro says a long o sound.

audio.text: Watch: robot splits ro | bot. The first syllable is open, so ro says a long o sound.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: In farm, ar stays together because the r changes the vowel sound.

audio.text: Demo: In farm, ar stays together because the r changes the vowel sound.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: reread has re- plus read. The prefix re- means again, so reread means read again.

audio.text: Demo: reread has re- plus read. The prefix re- means again, so reread means read again.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Practice: hopeful is built from hope plus -ful. The suffix -ful means full of.

audio.text: Practice: hopeful is built from hope plus -ful. The suffix -ful means full of.
audio.label: Listen

hint_ladder: Look for the word parts before you guess.
Mark the vowel pattern or meaningful part.
Read each chunk, then blend and check that it makes sense.

guided_practice.prompt: Which syllable break shows an open syllable first in robot?

Mark the vowel pattern or meaningful part.
Read each chunk, then blend and check that it makes sense.
guided_practice.explanation: The answer is ro-bot. Read the helpful chunks first, then blend the whole word.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — ro-bot is the decoding clue that fits.
guided_practice.clap_prompt: Read robot one chunk at a time: ro | bot.

misconception_bank.syllable_break_error: Learner may split a word at an unhelpful place or miss whether the syllable is open or closed.
misconception_bank.r_controlled_vowel_confusion: Learner may separate the vowel from r or read ar, er, ir, or, ur as a plain short vowel.
misconception_bank.affix_meaning_confusion: Learner may read a prefix or suffix as random letters instead of a meaningful word part.
misconception_bank.multisyllable_guessing: Learner may guess from the first letters instead of decoding each syllable or morpheme.

game_theme.mission_intro: Unlock each library shelf by breaking big words into readable, meaningful chunks.

story.text: Listen to this page. This mission helps you read longer words with confidence. Look for syllables, r-controlled vowels, prefixes, suffixes, and useful word parts. Break the word into parts, read each part, then blend the whole word. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches Multisyllable Word Reading and Advanced Phonics. Look at the examples and notice how the word parts help you read. Try saying each chunk slowly before you blend it. A common mistake is guessing from the first letters only. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page shows a worked reading example. Look at the word and notice the syllable break or word part. Step one, find the chunks. Step two, read each chunk. Step three, blend and check that the word makes sense. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo helps you practice advanced phonics with support. Look for vowel patterns, r-controlled parts, prefixes, and suffixes. Read the parts in order, then blend the word smoothly. Do not guess before checking all the parts. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Use the word parts and the visual model to help you think. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Use Listen when you want to hear an important word or sentence. Choose or write your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. The word may have more than one helpful part, so slow down and look carefully. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Use Listen to hear the word or sentence when it helps. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show how you read multisyllable words and word parts. Break the word into chunks, read each chunk, and blend the word. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose or write the answer when ready.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your work as a stronger reader. Look at your stars and remember the decoding strategies you used. Reading longer words gets easier when you use word parts. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your reading growth. Look at your accuracy, hints, and next steps. If a level needs more work, continue to Skill Practice and read the words again. Use your results to choose what to practice next.
profile.label: Read This Page
